page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the ecs.g9i family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a baseline frequency of 3.2 GHz. The instance features 128 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other virtual machines on the KVM hypervisor.

The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 36 Gbps. However, this server type does not include any local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage, and workloads requiring hardware acceleration are not supported.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g9i.32xlarge offers a high-density, non-hyperthreaded CPU profile that is efficient for workloads requiring predictable, dedicated compute resources. The balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio makes it appropriate for enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, and distributed databases that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.2 GHz, the instance provides 128 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, ensuring consistent compute performance without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 36 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring reliance on network-attached storage. The dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile make it suitable for enterprise web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and distributed databases.
